title: Ready, Steady, Go! Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.
rounds.explanation: head: The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To jump on one foot.
rounds.explanation: hop: To jump on one foot.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The clear drink your body needs every day.
rounds.explanation: water: The clear drink your body needs every day.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The part from your shoulder to your hand.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: To push yourself off the ground with both feet.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: To rest with your eyes closed at night.

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: I wear a hat on my head when it is sunny.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: I hold my pencil with my fingers.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The children hop like little rabbits.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To move your hand from side to side to say hello or goodbye.
rounds.explanation: wave: To move your hand from side to side to say hello or goodbye. I wave goodbye to my friend.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'hop'?
rounds.explanation: hop: To jump on one foot. The children hop like little rabbits.

rounds.prompt: We ____ over the puddles when it rains.
rounds.explanation: jump: To push yourself off the ground with both feet. We jump over the puddles when it rains.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'hand'?
rounds.explanation: I wash my hands before I eat.

rounds.prompt: I hold my pencil with my ____s.
rounds.explanation: I hold my pencil with my fingers.

rounds.prompt: Which spelling is correct?
rounds.explanation: My tummy feels full after lunch.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 — Naming Body Parts
Speaking Game — Simon Says
Speaking 5 - Healthy Habit Interview
rounds.target: and answer six questions about moving, food, water and sleep.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: I nod my head when I understand.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: I drink water when I feel thirsty.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'clap' mean in this unit?
choices: To hit your hands together to make a sound.
The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.
The part from your shoulder to your hand.
rounds.answer: To hit your hands together to make a sound.
rounds.explanation: clap: To hit your hands together to make a sound.

rounds.prompt: What does 'energy' mean in this unit?
choices: The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.
The part from your shoulder to your hand.
The power you get from food and rest to play and learn.
rounds.answer: The power you get from food and rest to play and learn.
rounds.explanation: energy: The power you get from food and rest to play and learn.

rounds.prompt: What does 'arm' mean in this unit?
choices: One of the five long parts at the end of your hand.
The part from your shoulder to your hand.
The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.
rounds.answer: The part from your shoulder to your hand.
rounds.explanation: arm: The part from your shoulder to your hand.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'healthy'?
rounds.explanation: healthy is used as a adjective in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'reach'?
rounds.explanation: reach is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'toe'?
rounds.explanation: toe is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: The part at the end of your arm, with fingers.
The front of your body below your chest (also called the stomach).

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: To hit your hands together to make a sound.
Moving your body to keep it strong and fit.
Having lots of power in your body.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: The power you get from food and rest to play and learn.
To move your body around to face another way.
To move your arms up and down like wings.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: I hold my pencil with my ____s.
rounds.explanation: I hold my pencil with my fingers.

rounds.prompt: Which spelling is correct?
rounds.explanation: My tummy feels full after lunch.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: One of the five small parts at the end of your foot.
rounds.explanation: toe: One of the five small parts at the end of your foot. I can wiggle my toes inside my shoes.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: What does 'head' mean?
choices: The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.
The part from your shoulder to your hand.
The part at the end of your arm, with fingers.
One of the five long parts at the end of your hand.
rounds.answer: The top part of your body, with your eyes, ears, nose and mouth.
rounds.explanation: I wear a hat on my head when it is sunny.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The part from your shoulder to your hand.
rounds.explanation: I use my arms to carry my school bag.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'hand'?
rounds.explanation: I wash my hands before I eat.
